title: From Detection to Depletion: Cost-Exchange Limits in the Russia–Ukraine Drone War
title_normalized: from detection to depletion: cost-exchange limits in the russia–ukraine drone war

creators.name: Wu, Shaoyuan
creators.affiliation: Global AI Governance Research Center, EPINOVA LLC

publisher: Global AI Governance Research Center, EPINOVA LLC
description: This research report develops a Minimum Viable, Auditable framework for evaluating counter-drone sustainability in the Russia–Ukraine drone war. It shifts assessment from intercept-centric measures toward cost-exchange sustainability, loss suppression, and mission-outcome preservation, using indicators such as CER, OLER, CER*obs, CPLAobs, KAPR, and KAPS.
abstract: This research report examines cost-exchange limits in the Russia–Ukraine drone war and argues that unmanned aerial systems have shifted from tactical supplements to system-level drivers of military sustainability. Using a Minimum Viable, Auditable analytical baseline grounded in public data and conservative proxies, the report reframes counter-drone effectiveness away from intercept or shoot-down rates and toward sustainability- and mission-outcome-oriented evaluation. It integrates five indicators: Cost Exchange Ratio (CER), Observational Loss Suppression Ratio (OLER), composite cost-loss indicator CER*obs, Cost per Loss Avoided (CPLAobs), and Key Asset Preservation Ratio/Score (KAPR/KAPS). Applied to 2023–2025 public data, the analysis finds that high interception performance can coexist with deteriorating cost sustainability; that attack scale and saturation make unit cost curves decisive; and that mission outcomes such as power continuity and fuel supply preservation provide a more meaningful measure of strategic effect than interception counts alone. The report concludes that counter-UAS capability should be treated as an endurance capability under sustained saturation, requiring low-cost terminal layers, multi-sensor fusion, magazine-depth planning, and governance attention to AI-assisted detection and automated engagement.

dates.description: Public release of the research report.

figures.title: Observational Indicators (2023)
figures.description: Visualizes the 2023 evolution of observational indicators including CER, OLER, CER*obs, CPLAobs, and KAPS for Ukraine and Russia under the MVA baseline.
figures.source: Author’s MVA baseline calculations based on publicly available data and conservative proxy assumptions
figures.note: Illustrative trend visualization; values are bounded ranges and not statistical confidence intervals.

figures.title: Observational Indicators (2024)
figures.description: Visualizes the 2024 evolution of observational indicators including CER, OLER, CER*obs, CPLAobs, and KAPS for Ukraine and Russia under the MVA baseline.
figures.source: Author’s MVA baseline calculations based on publicly available data and conservative proxy assumptions
figures.note: Illustrative trend visualization; values are bounded ranges and not statistical confidence intervals.

figures.title: Observational Indicators (2025)
figures.description: Visualizes the 2025 evolution of observational indicators including CER, OLER, CER*obs, CPLAobs, and KAPS for Ukraine and Russia under provisional MVA baseline assumptions.
figures.source: Author’s MVA baseline calculations based on publicly available data and conservative proxy assumptions
figures.note: 2025 values are provisional and included for temporal continuity under the MVA standard.

figures.title: Sustainability Decision Frontier under Drone Saturation: CPLAcf vs. CER*cf
figures.description: Plots counterfactual 2025 sustainability trade-offs across defense-structure choices under fixed stress assumptions, comparing marginal cost per loss avoided against composite counterfactual cost-loss pressure.
figures.note: Scenario-based stress-test visualization only; not an observed outcome or normative optimal configuration.

tables.title: Observational Sustainability and Mission Outcomes (MVA Baseline)
tables.description: Reports baseline observational indicators for Ukraine and Russia from 2023 to 2025, including Ntotal, Nattack, defense mix, CER, OLER, CER*obs, CPLAobs, and KAPS.
tables.source: Author’s MVA baseline calculations using public sources, critical-asset functional-loss proxies, and bounded sensitivity assumptions
tables.note: Attack-intensity values are not numerically symmetric across sides because they reflect different attack modes: high-frequency saturation versus low-frequency node targeting. 2025 values are provisional.

tables.title: Unified Counterfactual Assumptions
tables.description: Defines the low, base, and high stress multiplier scenarios used in the 2025 counterfactual stress test.
tables.note: Counterfactual assumptions are for comparative stress testing and do not represent observed outcomes.

tables.title: Counterfactual Stress Test Results (2025)
tables.description: Compares 2025 counterfactual stress-test results across SAM-heavy, balanced, and low-cost defense mixes, reporting CER, CLER, CER*cf, and CPLAcf ranges.
tables.note: Results are scenario-based and should not be interpreted as empirical measurements.

tables.title: Decision Rules for Interpreting Counter-Drone Performance under Saturation
tables.description: Provides a structured interpretation matrix linking combinations of CER, OLER, and KAPS to strategic interpretation and policy implications.
tables.note: Heuristic interpretation aid designed to prevent single-indicator misreading under saturation conditions.
